file_insights.changes_summary: Added ProductVariant and ProductModifier models with comprehensive documentation and helper methods
key_additions: Helper properties on Product model (variant_count, modifier_count, has_variants, has_modifiers)
Currency formatting and price calculation methods

patterns_discovered.description: Both ProductVariant and ProductModifier use SoftDeleteMixin and SoftDeleteManager for safe deletion without data loss

patterns_discovered.pattern_name: Foreign Key Cascade with Related Names
patterns_discovered.description: Product FK relationships use CASCADE on_delete with explicit related_name for reverse queries
All new models use UUID as primary key for global uniqueness and consistency
Models define multiple database indexes for common query patterns (product lookup, sorting, filtering)
Consistent formatted_price property using currency symbols dictionary with fallback
Both models provide organization property via product for multi-tenant filtering

gotchas_discovered.description: Documentation emphasizes that EVERY query MUST filter by organization via product lookup to maintain multi-tenancy
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Include organization context in all querysets when using these models

gotchas_discovered.description: ProductVariant stores absolute price while ProductModifier stores additional cost. ProductModifier defaults to 0 while ProductVariant requires explicit price
gotchas_discovered.location: price field definitions in both models
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Different semantics require careful handling in order calculation and display logic

gotchas_discovered.description: Only one variant per product should be marked as is_default, but this is not enforced at database level
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: set_as_default() method handles clearing other defaults, but manual updates could violate this constraint

gotchas_discovered.description: is_required flag indicates customer must select modifier, but enforcement happens in business logic, not database
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Application layer must validate that required modifiers are selected in orders

gotchas_discovered.description: Both models use CASCADE on Product delete, which will remove all variants/modifiers without soft delete safety
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider CASCADE behavior when deleting products; soft deletes on models don't protect from parent deletion

approach_outcome.description: Successfully implemented ProductVariant and ProductModifier models with complete documentation, helper methods, database indexes, and soft delete support
key_strengths: Extensive inline documentation with usage examples
Proper soft delete integration with dual managers
Optimized database indexes for common queries
Methods for state management (set_as_default, set_as_required, etc.)
testing_recommendations: Verify unique default variant constraint per product
Test cascade delete behavior on product deletion
Validate soft delete queries filter correctly
Ensure organization filtering is applied in views/serializers
Test price formatting across different currencies

recommendations.recommendation: Add database constraint to enforce single is_default variant per product (unique constraint with conditional index)
recommendations.rationale: Current design relies on application logic; database-level enforcement prevents accidental violations

recommendations.recommendation: Create custom queryset methods for common patterns (active_variants, required_modifiers, available_only)
recommendations.rationale: Reduces code duplication in views and serializers; ensures consistent filtering

recommendations.recommendation: Create model factories and fixtures for ProductVariant and ProductModifier in tests
recommendations.rationale: Enables thorough testing of soft delete behavior, pricing calculations, and state transitions

recommendations.recommendation: Add migration notes explaining the cascade delete behavior and soft delete implications
recommendations.rationale: Future developers need clear guidance on deletion semantics to avoid data loss

recommendations.recommendation: Consider validation in set_availability(), set_as_required(), and update_max_quantity() methods for production constraints
recommendations.rationale: Methods currently perform updates without business rule validation (e.g., can't disable only available variant)

recommendations.recommendation: Add caching for variant/modifier counts on Product model in high-traffic scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation queries database each time; caching could improve API response times
